title: Sort condition operators
description: Given a sort condition with any DynamoDB-style operator (eq, ne, lt, lte, gt, gte, between, begins_with, contains), when evaluated against a value, it should return the correct boolean result. Handles edge cases: null values, numeric vs string comparison, empty strings. Spec: features/sort/sort_conditions.feature

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Working on Phase 1: Sort Conditions.

Progress so far:
- Gherkin spec already existed (features/sort/sort_conditions.feature) — 9 operators covered with Scenario Outlines, null handling, empty string edge cases
- Added Scenario Outline for null input across all 8 single-value operators (was only testing eq before; needed to reach 100% coverage)
- Python: sort.py implemented with Sort class, all 9 operators (_coerce helper for numeric string coercion). 100% coverage. Black + Ruff clean.
- Python step definitions: features/steps/sort_steps.py — handles empty-string edge cases with explicit steps since behave regex can't match empty quoted values
- Rust: sort.rs implemented with SortCondition enum + OrderedValue newtype (implements Ord for serde_json::Value). 100% coverage via unit tests.
- Rust cucumber steps added to tests/cucumber.rs using regex= attribute to handle parametric operators
- parity checker failing: regex= steps in Rust not matched by parity checker pattern

Next: fix parity checker to extract regex= raw-string patterns from Rust steps, then run make check.
